{
  "name": "Slice Rank Method",
  "seminal_paper": {
    "arxiv_id": "1605.09223",
    "doi": "10.4007/annals.2017.185.1.8",
    "citation": "J. S. Ellenberg and D. Gijswijt, Ann. of Math. (2) 185 (2017), no. 1, 339-343.",
    "year": 2017
  },
  "home_domain": "algebraic_combinatorics",
  "what_it_does": "The slice rank method is a polynomial-tensor technique for upper-bounding pattern-avoiding subsets of finite vector spaces. It is best known for giving exponential upper bounds for cap sets, i.e. subsets of F_q^n with no nontrivial three-term arithmetic progressions.",
  "input_requirements": [
    "an ambient finite vector space F_q^n or closely related finite algebraic product space",
    "a forbidden configuration expressible by low-degree polynomial equations or linear forms over a finite field",
    "a subset A whose pattern-avoidance property forces the associated pattern tensor to have essentially diagonal support",
    "finite-field polynomial dimension bounds for the relevant degree slices"
  ],
  "output_signature": "an upper bound on the maximum size or density of a subset avoiding the specified algebraic pattern",
  "input_match": [
    {
      "requirement": "an ambient finite vector space F_q^n or closely related finite algebraic product space",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match: the target objects are powerful numbers in ℕ, not points of F_q^n."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a forbidden configuration expressible by low-degree polynomial equations or linear forms over a finite field",
      "match": "PARTIAL",
      "evidence": "Set.IsAPOfLength captures the three-term arithmetic-progression condition, but not over a finite field."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a subset A whose pattern-avoidance property forces the associated pattern tensor to have essentially diagonal support",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match: the theorem asks finiteness of actual consecutive powerful-number triples, not avoidance by a large subset."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "finite-field polynomial dimension bounds for the relevant degree slices",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match: no finite-field polynomial degree-slice structure is present in the statement."
    }
  ],
  "known_obstructions": [
    "The integers lack the finite vector-space structure used by slice rank.",
    "Consecutiveness in the ordered sequence of powerful numbers is not an algebraic finite-field constraint.",
    "The target is an infinite-sequence finiteness question, while slice rank gives finite ambient-space density bounds.",
    "No tensor encoding with diagonal-support behavior is apparent from the Lean statement."
  ],
  "fit_score": 0.125,
  "source_analog_id": "A1"
}
